response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior individual contributor with 15+ years of development experience and a genuine affinity for testing and developer tooling, but this is a Level II people-management role and the resume shows no people-management or formal team-leadership experience at all. Technical leadership appears informal (workshops, open-source founder work) rather than sustained roles guiding engineers, setting roadmaps, or running performance processes, so the core requirements of the role are unmet.
strengths: 15+ years of software development across web, mobile, desktop, embedded, and low-latency systems
Authentic developer-tools and testing background: test frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), CI/CD infrastructure, Selenium training, MCP code generators
Mobile-platform exposure, including shipping and maintaining iOS/Apple TV apps and building a mobile device orchestration tool
concerns: No people-management experience, versus the required 2+ years for a manager role
No formal technical-leadership roles or evidence of roadmap ownership and organizational change leadership
Developer-tools work is spread across side projects and role fragments rather than 3+ years of dedicated build/test infrastructure ownership

key_factors.factor: Lack of formal technical leadership and roadmap ownership
